scope.family: logical Bell pairs with right mouths encoded into product five-qubit perfect-code blocks
scope.coupling_family: identity encoded-mouth decoder versus algebraically correct right-block decoder

core_claim: Low-order physical entropy/min-cut shadows through the code distance do not determine encoded fixed-port transmission; algebraic reconstruction predicts the correct block routing and explicit logical channel.

theorem_style_claims.claim: For right-mouth stabilizer encoding blocks of distance d, logical mouth twists are invisible to labeled physical entropy diagnostics through order d because all pairing-dependent Bell checks have support at least d+1.
For the declared identity right-block decoder, perfect named-port transmission equals the number of fixed points of the logical connectivity map; this is not a quantum-capacity formula.
The pairing-aware decoder recovers the exact full logical channel, while wrong declared decoders fail perfect transmission off fixed points.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: all labeled physical regions of size <=3 for two five-qubit right blocks plus two left mouths
all logical mouth permutations for m<=3 with product five-qubit right blocks
bounded_exhaustive_evidence.low_order_profile_classes_through_distan: one class for each fixed m in the declared atlas

proof_obligations.claim: Regression check for the Goal 11 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.low_order_physical_entropy_through_dista: insufficient for the encoded mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.encoded_block_entropy: detects the mouth map at decoder scale
diagnostic_hierarchy.algebraic_connectivity_matrix: sufficient to specify identity and pairing-aware block routing in the declared family

known_vs_new.conceptual_prior_art: Engelhardt-Liu 2023, Algebraic ER=EPR and Complexity Transfer, is the primary prior art for the broad operator-algebraic ER=EPR framing.
known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer distance, logical Bell stabilizers, Choi matrices, and Clifford Pauli transfer.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages the finite encoded-mouth bridge-channel layer where low-order physical entropy diagnostics are blind but algebraic reconstruction specifies an explicit decoder-dependent logical channel.

limitations: This is an exact finite stabilizer encoded-mouth theorem, not a continuum-gravity theorem, not a noisy approximate-QEC theorem, and not a many-body traversable-wormhole simulation. Full encoded-block diagnostics reveal the mouth map.
next_frontier: Replace product stabilizer blocks with a state-derived two-screen model and test whether reconstruction transitions match independently computed generalized-entropy saddles.
